title: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

description: Savor the rich, robust flavor of a perfectly seared New York Strip Steak, enhanced by a luscious garlic herb butter. This classic American dinner recipe delivers a tender, juicy steak with a golden-brown crust, making it an impressive yet simple meal. Prepare to indulge in a restaurant-quality experience right in your own kitchen.

instructions.title: Prepare the Steaks
instructions.text: Pat the New York Strip steaks completely dry with paper towels. Season generously all over with kosher salt and freshly cracked black pepper. Let them rest at room temperature for at least 30 minutes, or up to an hour, for more even cooking.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan
instructions.text: Heat a heavy-bottomed skillet, preferably cast iron, over medium-high heat until it is smoking slightly. Add the olive oil to the hot pan and let it shimmer.

instructions.title: Sear the Steaks
instructions.text: Carefully place the seasoned steaks in the hot pan. Sear for 2-4 minutes per side for a beautiful, golden-brown crust, depending on the thickness of your steak and desired doneness. Do not overcrowd the pan; cook in batches if necessary.

instructions.title: Add Garlic Butter Aromatics
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium-low. Add the cold unsalted butter cubes, smashed garlic cloves, fresh rosemary sprigs, and fresh thyme sprigs to the pan around the steaks.

instructions.title: Baste the Steaks
instructions.text: As the butter melts and the aromatics infuse, tilt the pan slightly and use a large spoon to continuously baste the steaks with the flavorful garlic herb butter for 2-3 minutes. This ensures even cooking and infuses them with incredible aroma.

instructions.title: Check Doneness and Rest
instructions.text: Use a meat thermometer to check for desired doneness (125-130°F for rare, 130-135°F for medium-rare, 135-140°F for medium). Remove steaks from the pan and transfer to a cutting board. Let them rest for 5-10 minutes, tented loosely with foil, before slicing to allow the juices to redistribute and finish cooking.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Slice the rested steaks against the grain into thick pieces. Drizzle with any remaining garlic butter from the pan. Serve immediately with your favorite American dinner sides like mashed potatoes or roasted asparagus.

tags: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter
tips: For an authentic American steakhouse sear, ensure your pan is screaming hot before adding the steak. This creates a beautiful, crusty exterior while keeping the interior juicy.
Use high-quality unsalted butter and fresh herbs for the best garlic butter flavor. If you dont have fresh rosemary or thyme, dried herbs can be used, but use half the amount.
Always let your steak rest for 5-10 minutes after cooking. This allows the juices to redistribute, resulting in a more tender and flavorful cut. Serve with roasted potatoes or a fresh green salad.
article:
New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter: A Classic American Dinner Recipe

There are few culinary experiences that evoke the simple pleasure and sophisticated taste of a perfectly cooked steak quite like a New York Strip. When elevated with the aromatic richness of garlic butter, it transcends a mere meal, becoming a memorable American dinner recipe that’s both comforting and luxurious. Imagine a thick, juicy steak, seared to a beautiful crust, then bathed in a fragrant, bubbling butter infused with fresh garlic and herbs. This isn't just a dish; it's a statement of delicious intent, promising a culinary journey right in your own home.

## What is New York Strip Steak?

The New York Strip steak, also known as Kansas City Strip, ambassador steak, or top loin, is a beloved cut of beef that holds a special place in American steakhouses and home kitchens alike. It originates from the short loin of the cow, a muscle that does very little work, resulting in a wonderfully tender steak.

What sets the New York Strip apart is its ideal balance of tenderness, robust beefy flavor, and a satisfying chew. While not as melt-in-your-mouth tender as a filet mignon, it boasts significantly more flavor, thanks to its excellent marbling—those flecks of fat interspersed throughout the muscle. This marbling melts during cooking, basting the meat from within, ensuring a juicy and flavorful bite every time. Typically sold boneless, a good New York Strip steak is usually 1 to 1.5 inches thick, making it perfect for achieving that coveted deep sear on the outside while remaining beautifully pink and tender on the inside. It’s a versatile cut, ideal for grilling, broiling, or, as we'll explore, pan-searing with a decadent garlic butter finish.

## Ingredients for Garlic Butter Steak

Crafting the perfect New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter doesn't require an overwhelming list of ingredients, but quality and freshness are paramount.

For the Steak:
*   2 New York Strip steaks (1 to 1.5 inches thick, about 12-14 oz each)
*   2 tablespoons high smoke point oil (such as avocado, grapeseed, or canola oil)
*   Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste

For the Garlic Butter:
*   4 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   4-6 cloves fresh garlic, minced (or thinly sliced)
*   2 sprigs fresh rosemary
*   2 sprigs fresh thyme

Optional additions for extra flair:
*   A pinch of red pepper flakes for a subtle kick
*   A splash of Worcestershire sauce for added umami
*   Fresh parsley, chopped, for garnish

Always start with good quality, fresh ingredients. Fresh garlic and herbs make a significant difference in the final aroma and taste of your garlic butter.

## How to Cook New York Strip Steak with Garlic Butter

Mastering the art of cooking a New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter at home is surprisingly straightforward, yielding results that rival your favorite steakhouse.

1.  **Preparation is Key:**
    *   Remove the steaks from the refrigerator about 30-60 minutes before cooking. This allows them to come closer to room temperature, ensuring more even cooking.
    *   Pat the steaks thoroughly dry with paper towels. This is a critical step; moisture on the surface will steam the steak instead of searing it, preventing that beautiful, flavorful crust.
    *   Generously season both sides of the steaks with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper. Don't be shy with the salt; it enhances the beef's natural flavor.

2.  **Searing the Steak:**
    *   Heat a heavy-bottomed skillet (cast iron is ideal) over high heat until it's screaming hot. Add the high smoke point oil and let it shimmer.
    *   Carefully place the seasoned steaks in the hot pan. You should hear a vigorous sizzle immediately.
    *   Sear for 2-3 minutes per side, undisturbed, until a deep, golden-brown crust forms. Avoid moving the steak during this time. For a thicker steak, you can sear the edges as well.

3.  **Basting with Garlic Butter:**
    *   Reduce the heat to medium. Add the unsalted butter, minced garlic, rosemary, and thyme sprigs to the pan.
    *   Once the butter has melted and the garlic becomes fragrant (about 30 seconds), tilt the pan slightly. Use a large spoon to continuously baste the steaks with the melted garlic butter, pouring it over the top surfaces.
    *   Continue cooking and basting for another 3-6 minutes, or until the steaks reach your desired internal temperature (see "Tips for the Perfect Steak" for temperature guide). Flip the steaks once or twice during this basting process to ensure even cooking and flavor distribution.

4.  **Resting:**
    *   Once cooked to your liking, immediately remove the steaks from the pan and transfer them to a cutting board.
    *   Tent loosely with aluminum foil and let them rest for 5-10 minutes. This crucial step allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, resulting in a more tender and juicy steak. Skipping this step will cause all the delicious juices to run out when you cut into it.

Slice against the grain, spooning any remaining garlic butter from the pan over the top, and serve immediately. Enjoy your perfectly cooked New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter!

## Tips for the Perfect Steak

Achieving steakhouse-quality results at home is within reach with a few key techniques:

*   **Bring it to Room Temperature:** As mentioned, letting the steak sit out for 30-60 minutes before cooking ensures it cooks more evenly from edge to center.
*   **Pat it Dry, Seriously Dry:** Moisture is the enemy of a good sear. A thoroughly dry surface allows for a beautiful, crusty exterior.
*   **Don't Skimp on Seasoning:** Salt and pepper are your steak's best friends. Season generously right before it hits the pan.
*   **Hot Pan, Quick Sear:** A blazing hot pan creates that irresistible crust. Don't overcrowd the pan; cook steaks one or two at a time if necessary to maintain high heat.
*   **Use a Meat Thermometer:** This is the most reliable way to ensure your steak is cooked to your preferred doneness.
    *   Rare: 120-125°F (remove at 115-120°F)
    *   Medium-Rare: 130-135°F (remove at 125-130°F)
    *   Medium: 135-140°F (remove at 130-135°F)
    *   *Remember, the internal temperature will rise by 5-10 degrees while resting (carryover cooking).*
*   **Rest, Rest, Rest:** This step is non-negotiable. It allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb the juices, preventing a dry, tough steak.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings

The rich, savory flavors of New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter pair beautifully with a variety of classic American sides, creating a truly satisfying meal.

Classic Side Dishes:
*   Creamy mashed potatoes or a hearty baked potato
*   Roasted asparagus or green beans almondine
*   Creamed spinach or a simple garden salad with a vinaigrette
*   Sautéed mushrooms or caramelized onions

For a more indulgent experience, consider a small dollop of compound butter (like blue cheese butter or truffle butter) on top of the rested steak.

Wine Pairings:
Full-bodied red wines are a natural complement. Think Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Malbec, or a robust Zinfandel. Their tannins and dark fruit notes stand up well to the richness of the beef and garlic butter.

Beer Pairings:
For beer enthusiasts, a stout, porter, or a malty amber ale would be excellent choices, offering a nice contrast or complementary richness to the steak.

## Variations & FAQs

The beauty of the New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter recipe lies in its adaptability and straightforward nature.

Variations:
*   **Herb Swap:** Experiment with different herbs like fresh oregano, chives, or even a bay leaf for subtle flavor changes.
*   **Spicy Kick:** Add a pinch of red pepper flakes to the garlic butter for a touch of heat.
*   **Compound Butter:** Prepare the garlic butter mixture ahead of time, roll it into a log, chill, and slice off discs to melt over the hot steak at the end.
*   **Grilling:** While this recipe focuses on pan-searing, New York Strip is also fantastic on the grill. Simply grill to your desired doneness, then melt the garlic butter over the hot steaks as they rest.

FAQs:
*   **Q: Can I use pre-minced garlic?** A: While fresh garlic is always recommended for superior flavor, pre-minced garlic can be used in a pinch. Just be mindful it might burn a bit faster.
*   **Q: My steak isn't getting a good crust. What am I doing wrong?** A: Ensure your pan is very hot and your steak is patted thoroughly dry. Don't overcrowd the pan, as this lowers the temperature.
*   **Q: Why is unsalted butter specified?** A: Unsalted butter allows you to control the overall saltiness of the dish more precisely. If using salted butter, reduce the amount of added salt.
*   **Q: How do I store leftovers?** A: Store any leftover steak in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days. Reheat gently to avoid drying it out.

Indulging in a perfectly prepared New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter is more than just dinner; it’s an experience. This classic American dinner recipe, with its rich flavors and satisfying textures, is guaranteed to impress and become a cherished part of your culinary repertoire. So, fire up that pan, embrace the sizzle, and prepare to enjoy a truly magnificent steak.
